medications.indication_rationale: Shortens a recurrent cold-sore episode when started early; source prescribed valaciclovir 2000mg twice daily for 1 day (single-day high-dose regimen for herpes labialis).
medications.dose_route_frequency: 2000mg orally twice daily for 1 day (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Prevents cracking/bleeding of the healing lesion; apply with a clean cotton bud.
Topical steroids can worsen/spread a herpetic lesion and are not indicated.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Wash hands after touching the lip; don't touch the eyes; discard the shared lip balm and toothbrush used since the tingle; avoid kissing/sharing utensils until fully crusted.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Prevents autoinoculation (especially to the eye) and transmission during viral shedding.

patient_education_points.point: This is a cold sore (herpes labialis) reactivated by stress and poor sleep. The antiviral works best started early and shortens the episode; it heals in about 7–10 days and can recur.
It's contagious until it fully crusts over. Wash your hands, don't touch your eyes, throw away lip balm/toothbrush used since it started, and avoid kissing and sharing cups/cutlery.
Go to urgent eye care / ED if your eye becomes red, painful or light-sensitive, or if you develop a rapidly spreading hot red facial rash, widespread blisters, or feel systemically unwell.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Healing; consider suppressive antiviral if frequent recurrences.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Eye symptoms, spreading facial cellulitis, widespread lesions or systemic illness — urgent eye care/ED.

must_include_items: Recognise herpes labialis and offer early oral antiviral
Ocular-HSV safety-net (red/painful/photophobic eye → urgent eye care)
Infection-control advice (contagious until crusted; avoid eye autoinoculation)
acceptable_alternatives: Any oral antiviral (valaciclovir/famciclovir/aciclovir); topical antivirals give modest benefit; comfort measures
errors_of_commission: Prescribing/recommending topical corticosteroid to the lesion
Prescribing oral antibiotics for an uncomplicated cold sore
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires recognising HSV labialis, offering early antiviral, and giving an ocular-HSV safety-net plus infection-control advice.
